def: An ongoing fee deducted from mutual fund assets to cover marketing, distribution, and shareholder service costs. This fee is baked into the fund expense ratio and reduces investor returns annually.
avoid: Choose no-load funds with zero 12b-1 fees, or buy index funds directly from providers, and review fund prospectuses before investing.

def: A one-time charge levied when opening a new account, service, or line of credit. Common with cell phone plans, credit cards, and utilities.
avoid: Ask providers to waive activation fees during sign-up promotions or loyalty programs; negotiate for a credit on your first bill.

def: A broad fee covering general overhead costs such as billing, record-keeping, and customer support. Often added to insurance policies, loans, and service contracts without itemization.
avoid: Request a detailed breakdown of administrative costs and negotiate to have them capped or waived; compare providers with lower admin fee structures.
